channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Zenith Bank of Nigeria Completes Full Takeover of Kenya’s Paramount Bank After Regulatory Clearance
item.description: Nigeria’s Zenith Bank Plc has finalised the purchase of all shares in Paramount Bank Limited, a move that got the green light from the Central Bank of Kenya (CBK) and the National Treasury in March 20...

item.title: Nicole Kidman Celebrates 'Margot's Got Money' Premiere in New York
item.description: Aussie icon Nicole Kidman has been receiving rave reviews and plenty of love from her celebrity friends as she promotes her latest TV series, 'Margot's Got Money Troubles,' at a New York City premiere...

item.title: Online Platforms Launch to Teach Nigerians Practical Tech Skills
item.description: For Naija people wey dey hustle for better work opportunities, learning tech skills don become necessary. Odurinde Online Training don join hand with Haba Naija to make tech education easy and afforda...

item.title: New York Times investigation points to Adam Back as possible Satoshi Nakamoto
item.description: A New York Times investigation by John Carreyrou says British cryptographer Adam Back is the strongest circumstantial candidate yet for being Satoshi Nakamoto, the pseudonymous creator of Bitcoin. The...

item.title: Dan Levy’s Big Mistakes explores messy family bonds and moral chaos in a pastor’s downfall
item.description: Dan Levy’s new Netflix series, Big Mistakes, marks his return to television after the huge success of Schitt’s Creek. Co-created with Rachel Sennott, the show follows Nicky, a jittery pastor played by...

item.title: SB19’s ‘Wakas’ earns top 10 spot on Billboard World Digital Song Sales chart
item.description: SB19, one of the Philippines’ most successful and globally recognized P-pop groups, has achieved another milestone as their latest single ‘Wakas’ debuted at No. 9 on the prestigious Billboard World Di...

item.title: EFF opts for ward-based electoral system over mayoral candidate ahead of Joburg elections
item.description: The Economic Freedom Fighters (EFF) leader, Julius Malema, has announced that the party will not be campaigning for a specific mayoral candidate in Johannesburg ahead of the 2026 local government elec...

item.title: Rawalpindi Board Delays Matric Exams Due to US-Iran Talks in Capital
item.description: The Rawalpindi Board of Intermediate and Secondary Education (BISE) has announced the postponement of matriculation exams that were supposed to take place on April 10, 2026. The exams at 64 centers ac...

item.title: FG adjusts Lagos-Calabar coastal highway route to include Ogoniland in memory of Senator Mpigi
item.description: The Federal Government don show respect to the late Senator Mpigi Barinada Barry by approving the rerouting of the Lagos-Calabar Coastal Highway make e pass through Ogoniland. Minister of Works, David...

item.title: Canadian Auction House Corrects Centuries-Old Misattribution in Hudson’s Bay Portrait
item.description: When Heffel Fine Art Auction House prepared to sell the second batch of artworks from Hudson’s Bay Company (HBC), staff were captivated by a portrait of Prince Rupert. Initially attributed to the stud...

item.title: Bronson Xerri speaks about his return to first grade after a hectic month
item.description: Bulldogs player Bronson Xerri has shared his thoughts on a whirlwind month following his return to first-grade rugby. The 2026 season has seen a tumultuous journey for Xerri, who had to overcome a ser...

item.title: DA Investigates Over 1,600 Delayed Court Judgments in South Africa
item.description: The Democratic Alliance (DA) is set to investigate the overwhelming backlog of 1,657 reserved court judgments, which have yet to be handed down by South Africa's higher courts. The issue was brought t...

item.title: Australian War Memorial responds to Ben Roberts-Smith arrest amid media scrutiny
item.description: Shortly after news broke that Ben Roberts-Smith had been arrested over alleged war crime murders, the Australian War Memorial (AWM) issued a media alert. The situation has placed the AWM in a difficul...

item.title: Telangana HC to decide on Pawan Khera's plea against Assam Police FIR
item.description: The Telangana High Court on Thursday reserved its order on a plea by Congress leader Pawan Khera seeking protection from arrest in connection with an FIR filed by Assam Police. The FIR was based on a ...

item.title: Banklink Africa invests extra ₦2bn in CMFC to boost mining finance in Nigeria
item.description: Banklink Africa Private Equities Limited don drop another ₦2 billion inside Critical Minerals Financing Corporation Plc (CMFC) to push the company’s plan to change from DEAP Capital Management into a ...

item.title: Zamfara Attack: Bandits Kill Police, Civilians, Eleven Assailants Neutralized
item.description: Wahala just burst for Zamfara state again! Bandits, plenty of them – over fifty riding on motorcycles – attacked Yarkatsinan Laka Community for Bungudu Local Government Area. Dem just go and kill five...

item.title: African Startups Lead with Advanced Homegrown Technology Solutions
item.description: Dar es Salaam: Africa dey experience major growth in innovation as local startups dey bring smart and sophisticated solutions to some serious problems across the continent. Qualcomm, through its Make ...

item.title: World Bank Revises India's FY27 GDP Growth Forecast to 6.6%, Cites West Asia Conflict Concerns
item.description: The World Bank has revised its growth projections for India for the fiscal year 2026-27, lowering the GDP forecast from 7.2% to 6.6%. This adjustment comes in response to the ongoing conflict in West ...

item.title: Oil Prices Rise as Middle East Ceasefire Faces Uncertainty Amid Ongoing Strikes
item.description: Oil prices surged on Thursday while most global stock markets dipped, as investors grew wary about the sustainability of the recent Middle East ceasefire between the United States and Iran. Despite a ...

item.title: Musina Residents Struggle with Chronic Water Shortages, Elderly and Disabled Most Affected
item.description: In Musina, Limpopo, chronic water shortages are severely affecting elderly and disabled residents, who often have to rely on limited resources to access water. 100-year-old Judas Tlou uses his old-age...
